concept_name: Parallel-Plate Capacitor Field — Uniform E = V/d Between the Plates, ≈0 Outside
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.2 Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance (§2.12 Parallel plate capacitor) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. HC Verma consulted for SEQUENCE (set up two oppositely charged plates with a gap d, switch on V and show the field fill the gap in dead-straight, parallel, equally-spaced lines from + to − — not the radial fan a point charge would give, E = V/d → probe three points (near +, dead centre, near −) and show all three live E readouts identical: the field is UNIFORM → derive WHY from two charged sheets, each σ/2ε₀, ADDING inside to σ/ε₀ → turn edge-on and show the same two sheet-fields CANCELLING outside, confining the field to the gap with only a small edge fringe → hold V fixed and double d live, watch E halve: E ∝ 1/d → hand the explorer the V and d sliders plus a draggable test charge), never copied. The atomic idea: between two oppositely charged parallel plates the electric field is UNIFORM — straight, parallel, equally-spaced lines running + to −, the SAME magnitude E = V/d = σ/ε₀ at every interior point regardless of position; it is ≈0 outside the plates (the two sheet-fields cancel) with only a small edge fringe; and at fixed voltage E ∝ 1/d. SCOPE: this is the UNIFORM-FIELD diamond of Ch.2. It declares electric_field_point_charge (the radial 1/r² field it is NOT), gauss_law_sheet (the single isolated sheet's σ/2ε₀), and electric_potential_meaning (V = W/q) as prerequisites and does NOT re-teach them. It STOPS SHORT of capacitance C = ε₀A/d, the stored energy ½CV², dielectrics, and the energy density of the field. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; the three misconceptions are confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch as a straightforward contrast beat — no predict→reveal). Reconstructed 2026-07-08 from the original 7-state Socratic arc to the Rule 31/32 straightforward model: STATE_1+STATE_2 merged into one setup-and-switch-on hook, the old STATE_4 superposition derivation split across two states (inside-add, then outside-cancel edge-on) so each state carries exactly one idea, and all predict-pause/wait_for_answer wiring is stripped. Routed to the field_3d parallel_plates scenario (plate + uniform-field-line + three-labeled-probe-point + two-sheet-superposition + edge-on-fringe + gap-widen + draggable-test-charge primitives — the field_3d_config below is the renderer contract).

real_world_anchor.primary: Open the regulator knob box of an old ceiling fan and you find a small capacitor: two flat metal sheets held a tiny even gap apart. Put a voltage across them and the space in that gap fills with an electric field that is the SAME everywhere inside — straight, even, edge to edge. That perfectly even field is exactly what we build here: two plates, one small uniform gap.
real_world_anchor.secondary: The screen of your smartphone is a stack of two thin conducting layers separated by a whisker-thin gap. Your fingertip changes the voltage across them, and the touch sensor reads the uniform field in that gap. Two parallel conducting sheets, an even field between — the same physics as the fan capacitor, shrunk onto glass.

sigma.name: Surface charge density on EACH plate

E.name: Electric field magnitude between the plates — uniform

formulas.direction: from the positive plate to the negative plate, perpendicular to both plates

constraints: The electric field between two oppositely charged parallel plates is UNIFORM: the same magnitude and direction at every interior point (C1).
The field lines are straight, parallel, equally spaced and perpendicular to both plates, running from the positive plate to the negative plate (C2).
The field magnitude E does NOT depend on position between the plates — it is independent of how near or far you are from either plate (C3).
Outside an ideal pair of plates the field is approximately zero; only a small fringe field curls around the very edges (C4).
Each plate acts as a charged sheet producing a uniform field of magnitude sigma/(2*epsilon0) on each side (C5).
Between the plates the two sheet-fields point the SAME way and ADD; outside they point OPPOSITE ways and CANCEL (C6).
Inside the plates E = V/d = sigma/epsilon0 — the two expressions agree (C7).
At fixed voltage V, the field is inversely proportional to the separation: E ∝ 1/d (doubling d halves E) (C8).
At fixed separation d, the field is proportional to the voltage: E ∝ V (C9).

aha_moment.statement: The field is the same at every point between the plates.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Three labeled points (A, B, C) at the +edge, dead centre, and −edge each show a live E readout — all three numbers identical.

STATE_1.title: Two plates, one gap — switch on V

scene_composition.text: Two plates, gap d, connect voltage V
the gap between them is d
field fills the gap — straight, parallel lines

tts_sentences.text_en: Two flat metal plates face a small gap — like the ceiling-fan regulator capacitor's plates.
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు చదునైన మెటల్ plates ఒక చిన్న gap ఎదురుగా ఉన్నాయి — ceiling-fan regulator capacitor యొక్క plates లాగా.

tts_sentences.text_en: That gap between them is the plate separation, d.
tts_sentences.text_te: వాటి మధ్య ఉన్న ఆ gap నే plate separation, d అంటారు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Connect a voltage V across the plates.
tts_sentences.text_te: plates మధ్య ఒక voltage V ను కనెక్ట్ చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: You might expect the field to fan out like a single charge's — instead it fills in dead-straight, parallel, evenly-spaced lines, running plate to plate.
tts_sentences.text_te: single charge దగ్గర ఉన్నట్టు field బయటకు విస్తరిస్తుందని మీరు అనుకోవచ్చు — కానీ బదులుగా ఇది పూర్తిగా straight, parallel, సమాన అంతరంలో ఉన్న lines తో నిండి, plate నుండి plate వరకు నడుస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The electric field between the plates fans out radially in every direction, the way it does around a single point charge.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The narration names this expectation, then the field lines draw in dead-straight, parallel, evenly-spaced lines running plate to plate — no fan, no curve.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Between parallel plates the field is uniform, not radial — straight, parallel lines from + to −.

STATE_2.title: PRIMARY aha — three equal probes: the field is uniform

tts_sentences.text_en: Probe the field at three points: right at the positive plate, dead centre, and right at the negative plate.
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడు పాయింట్ల దగ్గర field ను probe చేయండి: positive plate దగ్గర సరిగ్గా, సరిగ్గా మధ్యలో, మరియు negative plate దగ్గర సరిగ్గా.

tts_sentences.text_en: You might expect it stronger near the plates and weaker in the middle — all three readings return identical.
tts_sentences.text_te: plates దగ్గర ఇది బలంగా, మధ్యలో బలహీనంగా ఉంటుందని మీరు అనుకోవచ్చు — కానీ మూడు readings కూడా ఒకేలా వస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is what uniform means: E equals V over d, same everywhere inside.
tts_sentences.text_te: uniform అంటే ఇదే: electric field E equals voltage V over separation d, లోపల ప్రతిచోటా ఒకేలా ఉంటుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The field is stronger right next to the plates and weaker in the middle of the gap.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Three labeled points, A, B and C — near the positive plate, dead centre, and near the negative plate — each show an identical live E reading.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The field is the same at every interior point — that is what uniform means.

STATE_3.title: Why uniform — two sheets add inside

scene_composition.text: each plate = a sheet, field σ/2ε₀
sum = σ/ε₀ = V/d, the same field

tts_sentences.text_en: Treat each plate as a charged sheet, each one making a field of sigma over two epsilon-nought.
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి plate నూ ఒక charged sheet గా అనుకోండి, ప్రతి ఒక్కటి sigma over two epsilon-nought అనే field ను తయారు చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Between the plates both sheet-fields point the same way, so they add.
tts_sentences.text_te: plates మధ్య, ఈ రెండు sheet-fields ఒకే దిశలో ఉంటాయి, కాబట్టి అవి add అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The sum is sigma over epsilon-nought — the same uniform field, E equals V over d, we already saw.
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ మొత్తం sigma over epsilon-nought — అదే uniform field, electric field E equals voltage V over separation d, ఇది మనం ఇంతకుముందే చూశాం.

scene_composition.text: turn edge-on, look from the side
only a small fringe curls at the edges

tts_sentences.text_en: Turn the capacitor edge-on: the same two sheet-fields are here, but now look further out, beyond either plate.
tts_sentences.text_te: capacitor ను edge-on గా తిప్పండి: ఇక్కడ కూడా అవే రెండు sheet-fields ఉన్నాయి, కానీ ఇప్పుడు మరింత బయటకు, ఏదైనా plate కు ఆవల చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Out there the two fields point opposite ways, so they cancel to nearly zero.
tts_sentences.text_te: అక్కడ ఈ రెండు fields వ్యతిరేక దిశల్లో ఉంటాయి, కాబట్టి అవి దాదాపు జీరో కి cancel అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Only a thin fringe curls round the very edges — everywhere else outside, the field is essentially gone.
tts_sentences.text_te: plates అంచుల దగ్గర మాత్రమే కొద్దిగా curl అవుతుంది — బయట మిగతా ప్రతిచోటా, field దాదాపు పోయినట్టే.

STATE_5.title: Fixed V, double d — E halves

scene_composition.text: V pinned at 12 V — about to pull plates apart
gap widens, live E readout falls
E = V/d — double d, E halves

tts_sentences.text_en: Keep voltage V fixed at twelve volts — I am about to pull the plates twice as far apart.
tts_sentences.text_te: voltage V ను పన్నెండు volts వద్ద ఫిక్స్ గా ఉంచండి — ఇప్పుడు నేను plates ను రెండింతలు దూరం లాగబోతున్నాను.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the gap widen and the lines spread — the live reading falls from twelve hundred to six hundred volts per metre.
tts_sentences.text_te: gap వెడల్పు అవ్వడం మరియు lines విస్తరించడం చూడండి — live reading పన్నెండు వందల నుండి ఆరు వందల volts per metre కి పడిపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Since E equals V over d, doubling d at fixed voltage always halves the field.
tts_sentences.text_te: electric field E equals voltage V over separation d కాబట్టి, voltage ఫిక్స్ గా ఉన్నప్పుడు separation d ని రెట్టింపు చేస్తే, field ఎప్పుడూ సగం అవుతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The electric field depends only on the voltage, so moving the plates apart at fixed V should leave the field unchanged.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: With V pinned at 12 V, the plates pull apart and the live readout falls from 1200 V/m to 600 V/m.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Since E = V/d, at fixed voltage doubling the separation halves the field.

STATE_6.title: Explore — drag V, d, and the test charge

scene_composition.text: drag V and d — watch E = V/d update
drag the yellow test charge inside
drag it outside — the force vanishes

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive it — use the sliders for voltage V and separation d.
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు దీన్ని నడపండి — voltage V మరియు separation d కోసం sliders ను వాడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the live field update, and drag the yellow test charge — the force stays constant inside, gone outside.
tts_sentences.text_te: live field update అవ్వడం చూడండి, మరియు yellow test charge ను డ్రాగ్ చేయండి — force లోపల స్థిరంగా ఉంటుంది, బయట పోతుంది.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered the parallel-plate capacitor field when they can (1) state that the field lines between two oppositely charged parallel plates are straight, parallel and equally spaced, running from the positive plate to the negative plate; (2) recognise that the field is UNIFORM — the same magnitude E = V/d at every interior point, independent of position; (3) reason that at fixed voltage E ∝ 1/d, so doubling the separation halves the field; (4) state that the field outside an ideal capacitor is approximately zero, with only a small edge fringe; (5) derive E = σ/ε₀ between the plates by superposing two charged-sheet fields of σ/2ε₀ each (adding inside, cancelling outside); and (6) conclude that the force F = qE on a test charge is the same everywhere inside, because the field is uniform.

questions.stem: A voltage is connected across two parallel plates. What is the shape of the electric field lines between them?

options.A: Straight, parallel, equally-spaced lines running from the positive plate to the negative plate
options.B: Lines that fan out radially from each plate, like the field around a point charge
options.C: Curved lines that loop from one plate and return to the same plate
options.D: Lines that run along the plates, parallel to their surfaces

distractor_misconceptions.B: imports the radial point-charge picture instead of the straight, parallel plate picture
distractor_misconceptions.C: borrows the closed-loop look of magnetic field lines, which do not apply between charged plates
distractor_misconceptions.D: puts the field ALONG the plates instead of across the gap from + to −

questions.tested_idea: Between parallel plates the field lines are straight, parallel and equally spaced, from + to −.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two oppositely charged metal plates face each other with a voltage across them. Describe the geometry of the field lines in the gap.

questions.stem: You measure the electric field at three points between the plates — next to the positive plate, in the dead centre, and next to the negative plate. How do the three values compare?

options.A: All three are equal — the field is the same at every interior point
options.B: The field is strongest next to the plates and weakest in the centre
options.C: The field is strongest in the centre and weakest near the plates
options.D: The field falls off steadily from the positive plate to the negative plate

distractor_misconceptions.B: the confronted misconception — imagines the field is concentrated near the plates rather than uniform
distractor_misconceptions.C: inverts that wrong picture, putting the strong field in the middle instead
distractor_misconceptions.D: treats the gap like a 1/r decay away from the positive plate, ignoring uniformity

questions.tested_idea: The field between parallel plates is uniform — equal magnitude at every interior point.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A probe reads the field just inside the positive plate, halfway across, and just inside the negative plate of a capacitor. State how the three readings compare.

questions.stem: The voltage across a parallel-plate capacitor is held fixed while the plates are pulled twice as far apart. What happens to the field between them?

options.A: It halves, because E = V/d and d has doubled
options.B: It stays the same, because the field depends only on the voltage
options.C: It doubles, because the plates are now further apart
options.D: It drops to zero, because the plates are too far apart to influence each other

distractor_misconceptions.B: the confronted misconception — thinks E depends only on V, ignoring the d in E = V/d
distractor_misconceptions.C: gets the proportionality backwards, treating E as growing with d
distractor_misconceptions.D: imagines the field switching off entirely once the gap widens

questions.tested_idea: At fixed voltage E = V/d ∝ 1/d, so doubling the separation halves the field.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Keeping the battery voltage constant, you move the plates of a capacitor from 1 mm to 2 mm apart. State what happens to the field in the gap and why.

questions.stem: What is the electric field in the region OUTSIDE an ideal parallel-plate capacitor, beyond the plates?

options.A: Approximately zero — the two plates' fields cancel outside, leaving only a small edge fringe
options.B: The same uniform field as inside, spreading out into the whole room
options.C: Stronger than inside, because the field escapes the narrow gap
options.D: Half the inside field, because only one plate contributes outside

distractor_misconceptions.B: the confronted misconception — imagines the field leaking out into the surrounding space
distractor_misconceptions.C: thinks the confined field intensifies once it leaves the gap
distractor_misconceptions.D: misreads the cancellation as a halving rather than near-total cancellation

questions.tested_idea: Outside an ideal capacitor the two sheet-fields cancel, so E ≈ 0 except for an edge fringe.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Standing just beyond the edge of a charged parallel-plate capacitor, what electric field would you measure, and why?

questions.stem: Treat each plate as a charged sheet that alone produces a field of magnitude σ/(2ε₀). What is the field BETWEEN the plates, and why?

options.A: σ/ε₀, because the two sheet-fields point the same way between the plates and add
options.B: σ/(2ε₀), because only one plate's field exists between the plates
options.C: Zero, because the two sheet-fields cancel between the plates
options.D: 2σ/ε₀, because the two fields add and then double again across the gap

distractor_misconceptions.B: counts only one sheet, forgetting both plates contribute inside
distractor_misconceptions.C: swaps the regions — cancellation happens OUTSIDE, not between the plates
distractor_misconceptions.D: double-counts the superposition, inflating the result

questions.tested_idea: Between the plates the two σ/2ε₀ sheet-fields add to σ/ε₀; outside they cancel.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Each plate of a capacitor is a sheet making a field σ/2ε₀ on each side. By superposition, what is the field in the gap between the two plates?

questions.stem: A small positive test charge is placed at several points between the plates and the force on it is measured each time. How does the force compare from point to point?

options.A: The same size and direction everywhere between the plates, because the field is uniform
options.B: Larger near the plates and smaller in the middle, where the field is weaker
options.C: Larger in the middle, where the charge is equidistant from both plates
options.D: It changes direction depending on which plate the charge is nearer to

distractor_misconceptions.B: assumes a non-uniform field, so the force would vary with position
distractor_misconceptions.C: imagines the field peaking in the centre rather than being constant
distractor_misconceptions.D: thinks the force direction tracks the nearer plate, not the fixed + to − field

questions.tested_idea: In a uniform field F = qE is constant, so the force on a test charge is the same everywhere inside.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A positive test charge is moved around inside a parallel-plate capacitor. State how the force on it changes as it moves, and justify your answer from the field.

capacitor_defaults.scene_separation_per_state_units: demo units (3D scene), distinct from the physical d in metres

STATE_1.label: Setup — plates, voltage on, field fills the gap
STATE_1.caption: Voltage on — field fills

STATE_2.label: PRIMARY aha — three labeled points, matching E readouts: field is uniform
STATE_2.caption: Same field at every point

STATE_3.label: Why uniform — two sheets σ/2ε₀ add inside
STATE_3.caption: Inside: two fields add
STATE_3._engine_note_cancel_outside_at_ms_0: INTENTIONAL zone-suppression trick, NOT a typo. cancel_outside_at_ms:0 combined with sheet_fields_at_ms:5700 permanently pins the OUTSIDE-zone sheet arrows' fadeU multiplier to 0 for the entire state (fadeU reaches 0 at ms>=cancelStart+800=800, and the inside/outside opacity ramp itself never starts before sheet_fields_at_ms=5700>800). Net effect: only the 2 INSIDE-zone sheet arrows become visible; the 4 OUTSIDE-zone arrows never appear this state — that is the whole point (this state teaches ADD-inside only; CANCEL-outside is taught next state). See updateParallelPlatesFrame() in field_3d_renderer.ts (~line 4972-4993). Do NOT change cancel_outside_at_ms away from 0 here — that would let the outside arrows flash briefly, muddying the one-idea-per-state contract (Rule 31/32).

STATE_4.label: Edge-on — outside the two fields cancel, only a fringe remains
STATE_4.caption: Outside: fields cancel

STATE_5.label: Hold V, double d — gap widens, E readout drops to half
STATE_5.caption: Double d — E halves
STATE_5.formula_overlay: E = V / d — fixed V: E ∝ 1/d, doubling d halves E

STATE_6.label: Explore — sliders V and d, drag the test charge
STATE_6.caption: All yours
